---
name: validate-scenes
description: 'Validate consistency of all Godot scenes in SupremeGodot. Use after renaming, removing, or cleaning up scenes to catch duplicate UIDs, broken path references, orphaned files, and SceneManager/DebugScene mismatches.'
argument-hint: 'None required — runs against the full project'
---

# Validate Scenes

## When to Use
- After renaming, removing, or moving any scene or prefab
- After a cleanup pass on `Scenes/`
- Any time a Godot UID warning appears in the editor or debug output
- As a routine health check before committing scene-related changes

## Procedure

Work through each check in order. Report every finding grouped by check. At the end, summarise with a pass/fail per check.

---

### Check 1 — Duplicate UIDs

Search all `.tscn` files under `godot/supreme-godot/` for their `uid=` value on the `[gd_scene]` line.

```
grep pattern: uid="uid://[^"]+"  in  **/*.tscn
```

- Collect every `(uid, filePath)` pair.
- Flag any UID that appears in more than one file as a **duplicate**.
- A duplicate means two files are copies of each other — one must be deleted or the UID regenerated by opening the file in Godot.

---

### Check 2 — Broken `path=` references

Search all `.tscn` files for `ext_resource` lines containing `path="res://..."`.

For each reference, resolve `res://` to `godot/supreme-godot/` and verify the file exists on disk.

- Flag any reference where the target file is missing as a **broken reference**.
- Include the referencing `.tscn` path and the missing target path in the report.

---

### Check 3 — Orphaned `.tscn` files (not in `Prefabs/`)

Find all `.tscn` files under `godot/supreme-godot/Scenes/` that are **not** under `Scenes/Prefabs/`.

For each, verify it has a `GameScene` enum entry in `godot/supreme-godot/Managers/SceneManager.cs` (check via `[ScenePath("res://...")]` attributes).

- Flag any navigable scene file with no matching `[ScenePath]` entry as **not registered**.

---

### Check 4 — `SceneManager.cs` enum vs. files on disk

Read `godot/supreme-godot/Managers/SceneManager.cs`.

For every `[ScenePath("res://...")]` attribute in the `GameScene` enum:
- Resolve the path and verify the `.tscn` file exists on disk.
- Flag any entry whose file is missing as a **dead enum value**.

Also verify a `GoTo{SceneName}()` method exists for every enum value. Flag missing methods.

---

### Check 5 — `DebugScene.tscn` button wiring

Read `godot/supreme-godot/Scenes/Debug/DebugScene.tscn`.

- Collect all `TargetScene = N` values from button nodes.
- Verify each integer `N` is a valid 0-based index into the `GameScene` enum.
- Flag any out-of-range or duplicate `TargetScene` values as a **wiring mismatch**.
- Verify the count of buttons equals the count of enum values (every scene should have a debug button).

---

### Check 6 — `.cs.uid` files for orphaned scripts

Search for `.cs.uid` files under `godot/supreme-godot/` where the corresponding `.cs` file no longer exists.

- Flag any stale `.cs.uid` file as **orphaned uid file**.

---

## Report Format

After all checks, output a table:

| Check | Status | Findings |
|-------|--------|----------|
| 1 — Duplicate UIDs | PASS / FAIL | list duplicates or "none" |
| 2 — Broken path refs | PASS / FAIL | list broken refs or "none" |
| 3 — Unregistered scenes | PASS / FAIL | list files or "none" |
| 4 — Dead enum values | PASS / FAIL | list values or "none" |
| 5 — DebugScene wiring | PASS / FAIL | list mismatches or "none" |
| 6 — Orphaned .cs.uid | PASS / FAIL | list files or "none" |

For each FAIL, also list the exact file paths and suggested fixes.
